howItWorks.intro: Sales tax is a percentage added to the purchase price of goods and services. This calculator works in three modes to solve any sales tax problem.
rules: Remove Tax: Pre-tax Price = Total Price ÷ (1 + Tax Rate ÷ 100)
Find Rate: Tax Rate = (Tax Amount ÷ Pre-tax Price) × 100
US sales tax varies by state, county, and city — rates range from 0% to 10%+
howItWorks.outro: Five states have no state sales tax: Alaska, Delaware, Montana, New Hampshire, and Oregon. However, some Alaska localities charge local sales tax. Always check combined state + local rates for accurate calculations.

faq.question: How do I calculate sales tax?
faq.answer: Multiply the price by the tax rate as a decimal. For example, $50 at 8% tax: $50 × 0.08 = $4.00 tax. The total is $50 + $4 = $54.00. This calculator does this automatically in 'Add Tax' mode.

faq.question: How do I find the pre-tax price from a total?
faq.answer: Divide the total price by (1 + tax rate as a decimal). For example, if you paid $54 with 8% tax: $54 ÷ 1.08 = $50.00 pre-tax price. Use 'Remove Tax' mode for this calculation.

faq.question: Which states have no sales tax?
faq.answer: Five states have no state-level sales tax: Alaska, Delaware, Montana, New Hampshire, and Oregon. However, Alaska allows local jurisdictions to charge sales tax, so some Alaska cities do have a local sales tax.

faq.question: Which state has the highest sales tax?
faq.answer: California has the highest state sales tax rate at 7.25%. However, when combined with local taxes, Tennessee and Louisiana often have the highest total rates, with some areas exceeding 10%.

faq.question: Is sales tax included in the listed price?
faq.answer: In the United States, sales tax is almost always added at checkout and not included in the displayed price. In most other countries, VAT (value-added tax) is included in the listed price.

faq.question: Do I pay sales tax on online purchases?
faq.answer: Yes, in most cases. Since the 2018 Supreme Court ruling in South Dakota v. Wayfair, states can require online retailers to collect sales tax even without a physical presence in the state. Most major online retailers now collect sales tax in all applicable states.

sections.title: What Is the Sales Tax Calculator?
sections.body: The Sales Tax Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your mode, price before tax, total price including tax, you get instant results including pre-tax price, tax amount, total price. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting pre-tax price right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your mode and need to find the right pre-tax price. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Mode
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your mode — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: price before tax, total price including tax, tax rate, tax amount. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your pre-tax price, tax amount, total price. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your mode before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both pre-tax price and tax amount — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your tax amount, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
